candidates.matchedPattern: Dynamic property assignment from user input

findings.title: normalizePathname() preserves leading '//', yielding a protocol-relative redirect target (root cause of the app-request.ts open redirect)
findings.description: normalizePathname() ensures a leading slash and strips trailing slashes (`absolutePathname.replace(/\/+$/, '')`) but never collapses or rejects leading double-slashes. For input `//evil.com/` it returns `{ pathname: '//evil.com', redirect: { pathname: '//evil.com', status: 308 } }`. This redirect struct is consumed by handleAppRequest (app-request.ts:18-20), which places `//evil.com` directly into the Location header — a protocol-relative URL the browser resolves to https://evil.com (open redirect). This file is the natural fix site: the normalized pathname is treated as a trusted same-origin redirect target downstream, but it can encode an arbitrary host. Same chain as the app-request.ts finding; reported here because the unsanitized value originates in this function.
findings.recommendation: Collapse leading slashes to a single '/' (e.g. `absolutePathname.replace(/^\/+/, '/')`) or reject pathnames matching `^//` so that no normalized/redirect pathname can ever be protocol-relative. Add a regression test for `//evil.com/` and `/\evil.com/`.
